directions.evidence: Pupils and irises sit high in the original eye apertures; chin and face pitch upward while the planted body stays frontal.
Pupils, nose tip, mouth, and face surface move toward screen-right, with the opposite cheek and ear more visible.
Pupils and eyelids aim downward; the chin tucks and the face pitches toward the bottom edge.
Pupils, nose tip, mouth, and face surface move toward screen-left, clearly opposing 090.
